prompt:
A 3D printer partially disassembled on a workbench after a severe nozzle clog: the print head carriage is opened or removed, revealing the hotend separated from the extruder, with a charred piece of filament and remnants of a “blob of death” nearby on a metal tray. Small tools—hex keys, screwdrivers, and pliers—are laid out, and a spare nozzle or hotend sits to one side, ready for replacement. The printer frame in the background makes it clear this is the same machine being repaired.

Near-future solarpunk workshop aesthetic with warm lighting and tidy but busy workbench vibes, plus a small plant in the background. Photorealistic 512×512 rendering, no brand logos or legible text. The image should clearly convey a deeper teardown required to fix or replace a clogged nozzle after a major failure.
